title: Octamethylcyclotetrasiloxane interacts with lithium ions
abstract: Provided is a method of extracting a metal ion, which comprises contacting a compound of formula (R 2 SiO) n with a source of the metal ion, whereby the compound and the metal ion form a complex, wherein each R is independently C1-10oalkyl, and n is 3-10.
claims: Claims What is claimed is: 1. A method of extracting a metal ion from a source, the method comprising: contacting a compound of formula (R2SiO) n with the metal ion in the source, whereby the compound and the metal ion form a complex; wherein each R is independently selected from Ci-ioalkyl, and n is 3-10. 2 The method of claim 1 , wherein the metal ion is a lithium ion. 3. The method of any one of claims 1-2, wherein the source comprises the metal ion and a counterion selected from the group consisting of F , CT, Br, P, HS04 , SO4 2 , HCO3 , CO3 2 , B(OH)4\ B2O5 4 , B3O7 5 , B4O9 6 , NO3 , H2PO4 , HPO4 2 , PO4 3 , and combinations thereof. 4. The method of any one of claims 1-3, wherein the compound is of formula (I): wherein each R 1 is independently selected from Cmalkyl. 5. The method of any one of claims 1-4, wherein the R 1 groups are the same. 6 The method of any one of claims 1-5, wherein the compound is 7. The method of any one of claims 1-6, wherein the source of the metal ion comprises an aqueous solution. 8. The method of any one of claims 1-7, wherein the source of the metal ion comprises seawater. 9. The method of any one of claims 1-8, wherein the pH of the source of the metal ion is 1 to 12. 10. The method of any one of claims 1-9, wherein the pH of the source of the metal ion is 2 to 4. 11. The method of any one of claims 1-11, further comprising pre-treating the source of the metal ion before contacting the compound of formula (I) with the source. 13. The method of any one of claims 1-12, further comprising isolating the metal ion from the complex. 14. The method of any one of claims 1-13, further comprising distilling the compound. 15. The method of any one of claims 1-14, wherein the concentration of the metal ion in the source is about 1 x 10 5 M to about 1 x 10 1 M. 16. The method of any one of claims 1-15, wherein the concentration of the metal ion in the source is about 1 x 10 5 M to about 1 x 10 3 M. 17. The method of any one of claims 1-16, wherein the concentration of the metal ion in the source is about 1 x 10 5 M to about 1 x 10 4 M. 18. The method of any one of claims 1-17, wherein the volume ratio between the compound and the source of the metal ion is about 1 : 100 to about 1 : 1. 19. The method of any one of claims 1-18, wherein the volume ratio is about 1:50 to about 1 :5. 20. The method of any one of claims 1-19, wherein the volume ratio is about 1:50 to about 1 : 10.
